methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $72M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 20.6x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Positron AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series B), post-money $1B+

data.narrative:
Lumotive is a Series B semiconductor company that has raised $72M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 8 private peers in chips and semiconductors—including Etched ($5.0B Series A), d-Matrix ($2.0B Series A), Positron AI ($1.0B Series B), and Substrate ($1.0B Series A)—alongside 3 public semiconductor benchmarks (ALAB, NVDA, AMD). The peer median valuation stands at $1.0B, with a range of $500M to $5.0B.

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis uses the $72M total raised as a proxy base and applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yielding $137M. Peer-median anchoring across the 6 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables implies $1.0B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples (median 20.6x across the three public tickers) cannot be applied due to the absence of Lumotive's revenue on file, which is standard for private companies at this stage.

The triangulated analysis across the two applicable methods yields a low of $116M, a median of $568M, and a high of $1.1B. The wide spread reflects tension between backward-looking capital raised metrics and forward-looking peer valuations in a sector experiencing significant capital concentration. Key uncertainties include Lumotive's revenue trajectory, customer pipeline depth, and competitive positioning relative to the higher-valued peers in the sample.
